author: @bobweiram6321
text: A 3 1/2" disk normally has one of the bottom corners cut at 45 degrees. There's also a write protection notch on the upper right edge. Overall, it's cool.

author: @RocketSteve
text:
Very awesome and inspirational build. Coming up with novel furniture designs is hard. At least you didn't just refer to this as the 'save icon', lol.
As an upgrade or future build how about placing a disc inside that has trays? Slide metal cover open and rotate inside disc to select tray of hidden goodies...

text:
So interesting I love the hidden compartment.
The ideal place to hide the Malteasers lol.

author: @gilbertsprojects2954
text: I made a similar coffee table when I was a teenager using some old scraps of wood and the front glass panel from a fruit machine (I think I got that from the local tip if I remember correctly...) and I put some of my favourite photos behind the clear sections.

author: @gilbertsprojects2954
text: That’s given me an even better idea using the same design; a “whiteboard” where the floppy disk label is the actual whiteboard part and you can put your pens/wiper etc in the hidden compartment. Basically very similar to the top part of the coffee table but thinner. Awesome 👍🏼

author: @iainwade
text:
Well done. It’s an interesting design idea, you took it through to completion, and made it to a high standard. A great project that you can be proud of.

I’m normally my own harshest critic: always seeing flaws or thing to improve in my work, but I don’t post to YouTube to benefit from the wisdom of all these armchair experts on here.

author: @AlphasysNl
text:
Things wrong with this build: 45 degree corner notch missing. Metal plate is not recessed/flush with table surface. Metal plate should have rounded corners. Metal plate is not spring loaded. Missing write protect notch. Dowels sticking out of the surface, is just wrong. A table should be flat. That Doom art would have been better in color. Most prevalent floppy color: Black.

Things I missed seeing: Sanding. Lots and lots of sanding. Always get your bonding layer on top of clean, sanded surface for maximum bond strength. Even just a bit of finger grease will leave spots that will flake over time. So lots of sanding and then cleaning with dilute ammonia solution to degrease and get rid of the wood dust. Yes, it stinks, but not for long.

Missed opportunities: Write protect notch could have been used to lock the lid or switch pockets on a rotating platform for more "secret storage". (Actually having a disc of some sort inside would have been even more floppy-like.)
